krzyzanowska2021.author: Krzyzanowska-Kowalczyk, J., Kowalczyk, M., Ponczek, M.B., Pecio, L., Nowak, P. and Kolodziejczyk-Czepas, J.
krzyzanowska2021.title: Pulmonaria officinalis and Pulmonaria obscura Extracts as Mitigators of Peroxynitrite-Induced Oxidative Stress and Cyclooxygenase-2 Inhibitors - In Vitro and In Silico Studies

actions.action: Traditional demulcent / expectorant for cough, bronchitis, sore throat and respiratory catarrh (mucilage and saponins)

constituents.name: Phenolic acids (rosmarinic, lithospermic and salvianolic acids)
constituents.note: The main antioxidant and COX-2-inhibiting constituents.

constituents.name: Mucilage, flavonoids, allantoin and silicic acid
constituents.note: Soothing and supporting constituents underlying the traditional respiratory use.

contraindications.note: As a member of the borage family (Boraginaceae), which can contain hepatotoxic pyrrolizidine alkaloids, prolonged or high-dose internal use is best avoided; use in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not recommended.
Traditional use only; clinical evidence in humans is limited, so a cough or respiratory complaint that persists needs medical assessment.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Molecules study of Pulmonaria antioxidant and COX-2-inhibitory activity). Lungwort is poorly covered by clinical trials, so respiratory actions remain needs-review. Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (cough, bronchitis, sore-throat); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

ema.author: European Medicines Agency (HMPC)
ema.title: European Union herbal monograph on Malva sylvestris L., folium and flos (Malvae folium / Malvae sylvestris flos)

actions.action: Demulcent for irritation of the mouth or throat and associated dry cough (soothing mucilage)
Soothes irritated and inflamed skin and mucous membranes
Mild laxative for occasional constipation (mucilage; traditional)

constituents.name: Mucilage polysaccharides
constituents.note: The high mucilage content gives the demulcent, soothing action.

constituents.name: Anthocyanins (malvin) and flavonoids
constituents.note: The blue-purple pigments and antioxidant constituents.

contraindications.note: Generally very well tolerated. Irritation of the mouth/throat or a cough that persists beyond about a week, or is accompanied by fever or breathlessness, needs medical assessment.
Use in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not recommended in the absence of adequate data.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from regulatory literature (EMA HMPC monograph for Malvae folium / flos). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (cough, sore-throat, skin-irritation, inflammation, constipation); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

ema.author: European Medicines Agency (HMPC)
ema.title: European Union herbal monograph on Althaea officinalis L., radix (Althaeae radix)

actions.action: Demulcent for irritation of the mouth or throat and associated dry cough (mucilage coats and soothes)
Eases mild stomach and gut discomfort, including heartburn / acid reflux and indigestion (protective mucilage)

constituents.name: Pectin and starch
constituents.note: Other carbohydrate constituents of the root.

contraindications.note: Very well tolerated. The mucilage can slow the absorption of other medicines, so take other drugs about an hour before or after marshmallow.
It may mildly lower blood sugar (caution alongside antidiabetic medicines). Use in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not recommended in the absence of adequate data.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from regulatory and phytotherapy literature (EMA HMPC monograph for Althaeae radix; ESCOP monograph). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (cough, sore-throat, indigestion, acid-reflux, skin-irritation); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

soleimani2019.author: Soleimani, V., Delghandi, P.S., Moallem, S.A. and Karimi, G.
soleimani2019.title: Safety and toxicity of silymarin, the major constituent of milk thistle extract: An updated review

fallah2021.author: Fallah, M., Davoodvandi, A., Nikmanzar, S., Aghili, S., Mirazimi, S.M.A., Aschner, M., Rashidian, A., Hamblin, M.R., Chamanara, M., Naghsh, N. and Mirzaei, H.
fallah2021.title: Silymarin (milk thistle extract) as a therapeutic agent in gastrointestinal cancer

actions.action: Hepatoprotective / liver support (protects liver cells, antioxidant and antifibrotic)
Supports liver recovery and 'detox' (traditional framing of the hepatoprotective action)

constituents.name: Silymarin (a flavonolignan complex)
constituents.note: The active complex; silybin (silibinin) is the most active component, with silydianin and silychristin.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.note: Additional antioxidant constituents of the seed.

contraindications.note: Very well tolerated, even at high doses; the main effects are mild gastrointestinal upset (nausea, loose stools). As a member of the daisy family (Asteraceae), it can cause allergic reactions in sensitive people.

Has a low potential for drug interactions (little effect on liver CYP enzymes), but use caution with medicines that have a narrow therapeutic window. Human pregnancy data are limited, so use cautiously.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Phytotherapy Research safety/toxicity review; Biomedicine & Pharmacotherapy review). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (liver-support, detox, indigestion, inflammation); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

soltani2021.title: A Review of the Phytochemistry and Antimicrobial Properties of Origanum vulgare L. and Subspecies

sharifirad2018.author: Sharifi-Rad, M., Varoni, E.M., Iriti, M., Martorell, M., Setzer, W.N., del Mar Contreras, M., Salehi, B., Soltani-Nejad, A., Rajabi, S., Tajbakhsh, M. and Sharifi-Rad, J.
sharifirad2018.title: Carvacrol and human health: A comprehensive review

actions.action: Antimicrobial (antibacterial and antifungal via carvacrol and thymol) - supports respiratory and gastrointestinal infections
Aromatic support for coughs and colds (traditional)
Carminative digestive for indigestion (traditional bitter aromatic)

constituents.name: Essential oil rich in carvacrol and thymol
constituents.note: Phenolic monoterpenes responsible for the strong antimicrobial activity.

contraindications.note: Culinary amounts are safe. Concentrated oregano essential oil is a strong irritant - dilute well before any topical use and do not take undiluted oil internally.

May lower blood sugar and has a mild antiplatelet effect, so use caution with antidiabetic or anticoagulant medicines; avoid medicinal/oil doses in pregnancy. Allergy is possible in people sensitive to the mint family (Lamiaceae).

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Iranian Journal of Pharmaceutical Research phytochemistry/antimicrobial review; Phytotherapy Research carvacrol review). Note: this is Origanum vulgare (wild marjoram), distinct from the book's Origanum majorana (sweet marjoram). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (cold, cough, indigestion, infection, inflammation); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.
